verses.latin_vulgate: Voce mea ad Dominum clamavi ;  voce mea ad Deum, et intendit mihi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קוֹלִ֣י אֶל־אֱלֹהִ֣ים וְאֶצְעָ֑קָה קוֹלִ֥י אֶל־אֱ֝לֹהִ֗ים וְהַאֲזִ֥ין אֵלָֽי׃
verses.king_james: I cried unto God with my voice, even unto God with my voice; and he gave ear unto me.

verses.latin_vulgate: In die tribulationis meæ Deum exquisivi ;  manibus meis nocte contra eum, et non sum deceptus.  Renuit consolari anima mea ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּי֥וֹם צָרָתִי֮ אֲדֹנָ֢י דָּ֫רָ֥שְׁתִּי יָדִ֤י ׀ לַ֣יְלָה נִ֭גְּרָה וְלֹ֣א תָפ֑וּג מֵאֲנָ֖ה הִנָּחֵ֣ם נַפְשִֽׁי׃
verses.king_james: In the day of my trouble I sought the Lord: my sore ran in the night, and ceased not: my soul refused to be comforted.

verses.latin_vulgate: memor fui Dei, et delectatus sum,  et exercitatus sum, et defecit spiritus meus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶזְכְּרָ֣ה אֱלֹהִ֣ים וְאֶהֱמָ֑יָה אָשִׂ֓יחָה ׀ וְתִתְעַטֵּ֖ף רוּחִ֣י סֶֽלָה׃
verses.king_james: I remembered God, and was troubled: I complained, and my spirit was overwhelmed. Selah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Anticipaverunt vigilias oculi mei ;  turbatus sum, et non sum locutus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָ֭חַזְתָּ שְׁמֻר֣וֹת עֵינָ֑י נִ֝פְעַ֗מְתִּי וְלֹ֣א אֲדַבֵּֽר׃
verses.king_james: Thou holdest mine eyes waking: I am so troubled that I cannot speak.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cogitavi dies antiquos,  et annos æternos in mente habui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חִשַּׁ֣בְתִּי יָמִ֣ים מִקֶּ֑דֶם שְׁ֝נ֗וֹת עוֹלָמִֽים׃
verses.king_james: I have considered the days of old, the years of ancient times.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et meditatus sum nocte cum corde meo,  et exercitabar, et scopebam spiritum meum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶ֥זְכְּרָ֥ה נְגִינָתִ֗י בַּ֫לָּ֥יְלָה עִם־לְבָבִ֥י אָשִׂ֑יחָה וַיְחַפֵּ֥שׂ רוּחִֽי׃
verses.king_james: I call to remembrance my song in the night: I commune with mine own heart: and my spirit made diligent search.

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid in æternum projiciet Deus ?  aut non apponet ut complacitior sit adhuc ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַֽ֭לְעוֹלָמִים יִזְנַ֥ח ׀ אֲדֹנָ֑י וְלֹא־יֹסִ֖יף לִרְצ֣וֹת עֽוֹד׃
verses.king_james: Will the Lord cast off for ever? and will he be favourable no more?

verses.latin_vulgate: aut in finem misericordiam suam abscindet,  a generatione in generationem ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֶאָפֵ֣ס לָנֶ֣צַח חַסְדּ֑וֹ גָּ֥מַר אֹ֝֗מֶר לְדֹ֣ר וָדֹֽר׃
verses.king_james: Is his mercy clean gone for ever? doth his promise fail for evermore?

verses.latin_vulgate: aut obliviscetur misereri Deus ?  aut continebit in ira sua misericordias suas ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲשָׁכַ֣ח חַנּ֣וֹת אֵ֑ל אִם־קָפַ֥ץ בְּ֝אַ֗ף רַחֲמָ֥יו סֶֽלָה׃
verses.king_james: Hath God forgotten to be gracious? hath he in anger shut up his tender mercies? Selah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixi : Nunc cœpi ;  hæc mutatio dexteræ Excelsi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָ֭אֹמַר חַלּ֣וֹתִי הִ֑יא שְׁ֝נ֗וֹת יְמִ֣ין עֶלְיֽוֹן׃
verses.king_james: And I said, This is my infirmity: but I will remember the years of the right hand of the most High.

verses.latin_vulgate: Memor fui operum Domini,  quia memor ero ab initio mirabilium tuorum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: (אזכיר) [אֶזְכּ֥וֹר] מַעַלְלֵי־יָ֑הּ כִּֽי־אֶזְכְּרָ֖ה מִקֶּ֣דֶם פִּלְאֶֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: I will remember the works of the Lord: surely I will remember thy wonders of old.

verses.latin_vulgate: et meditabor in omnibus operibus tuis,  et in adinventionibus tuis exercebor.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָגִ֥יתִי בְכׇל־פׇּעֳלֶ֑ךָ וּֽבַעֲלִ֖ילוֹתֶ֣יךָ אָשִֽׂיחָה׃
verses.king_james: I will meditate also of all thy work, and talk of thy doings.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus, in sancto via tua :  quis deus magnus sicut Deus noster ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֱ֭לֹהִים בַּקֹּ֣דֶשׁ דַּרְכֶּ֑ךָ מִי־אֵ֥ל גָּ֝ד֗וֹל כֵּאלֹהִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Thy way, O God, is in the sanctuary: who is so great a God as our God?

verses.latin_vulgate: Tu es Deus qui facis mirabilia :  notam fecisti in populis virtutem tuam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַתָּ֣ה הָ֭אֵל עֹ֣שֵׂה פֶ֑לֶא הוֹדַ֖עְתָּ בָעַמִּ֣ים עֻזֶּֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: Thou art the God that doest wonders: thou hast declared thy strength among the people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Redemisti in brachio tuo populum tuum,  filios Jacob et Joseph.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גָּאַ֣לְתָּ בִּזְר֣וֹעַ עַמֶּ֑ךָ בְּנֵֽי־יַעֲקֹ֖ב וְיוֹסֵ֣ף סֶֽלָה׃
verses.king_james: Thou hast with thine arm redeemed thy people, the sons of Jacob and Joseph. Selah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Viderunt te aquæ, Deus ;  viderunt te aquæ, et timuerunt :  et turbatæ sunt abyssi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רָ֘א֤וּךָ מַּ֨יִם ׀ אֱֽלֹהִ֗ים רָא֣וּךָ מַּ֣יִם יָחִ֑ילוּ אַ֝֗ף יִרְגְּז֥וּ תְהֹמֽוֹת׃
verses.king_james: The waters saw thee, O God, the waters saw thee; they were afraid: the depths also were troubled.

verses.latin_vulgate: Multitudo sonitus aquarum ;  vocem dederunt nubes.  Etenim sagittæ tuæ transeunt ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זֹ֤רְמוּ מַ֨יִם ׀ עָב֗וֹת ק֭וֹל נָתְנ֣וּ שְׁחָקִ֑ים אַף־חֲ֝צָצֶ֗יךָ יִתְהַלָּֽכוּ׃
verses.king_james: The clouds poured out water: the skies sent out a sound: thine arrows also went abroad.

verses.latin_vulgate: vox tonitrui tui in rota.  Illuxerunt coruscationes tuæ orbi terræ ;  commota est, et contremuit terra.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ק֤וֹל רַעַמְךָ֨ ׀ בַּגַּלְגַּ֗ל הֵאִ֣ירוּ בְרָקִ֣ים תֵּבֵ֑ל רָגְזָ֖ה וַתִּרְעַ֣שׁ הָאָֽרֶץ׃
verses.king_james: The voice of thy thunder was in the heaven: the lightnings lightened the world: the earth trembled and shook.

verses.latin_vulgate: In mari via tua, et semitæ tuæ in aquis multis,  et vestigia tua non cognoscentur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּיָּ֤ם דַּרְכֶּ֗ךָ (ושביליך) [וּֽ֭שְׁבִילְךָ] בְּמַ֣יִם רַבִּ֑ים וְ֝עִקְּבוֹתֶ֗יךָ לֹ֣א נֹדָֽעוּ׃
verses.king_james: Thy way is in the sea, and thy path in the great waters, and thy footsteps are not known.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deduxisti sicut oves populum tuum,  in manu Moysi et Aaron.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נָחִ֣יתָ כַצֹּ֣אן עַמֶּ֑ךָ בְּֽיַד־מֹשֶׁ֥ה וְאַהֲרֹֽן׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Thou leddest thy people like a flock by the hand of Moses and Aaron.
